chunks.text: Technical Reports - 1972. On Multimachine Power System Representations (M392) by A.R. Bergen and G. Gross. A Globally Converging Secant Method, with Applications to Boundary Value Problems (M374) by E. Polak. Computer Learning from English Text (M372) by Nagib A. Badre. Necessary and Sufficient Conditions for Stability for N-Input N-Output Convolution Feedback Systems With a Finite Number of Unstable Poles (M371) by Frank M. Callier and Charles A. Desoer. A Graph-Theoretic Approach to Linearly Orderable Sets (M369) by Kapali P. Eswaran. Introduction to Programming Science-Part I: Syntax and Semantics of Programming Languages (M368) by W.D. Maurer.
chunks.question: What are the titles and authors of the first six technical reports from 1972?
chunks.answer: Power systems, secant method, computer learning, stability conditions, graph theory, programming science.

chunks.text: Optimum Design of Integrated Variable-Gain Amplifiers (M367) by Willy M. C. Sansen. Zeros and Poles of Matrix Transfer Functions and Their Dynamical Interpretation (M366) by C.A. Desoer and J.D. Schulman. Martingales and Stochastic Integrals for Processes with a Two-Dimensional Parameter (M365) by Eugene Wong. An Improved Version of SLIC (M364) by Harold M. Kop. A Modified Markov Algorithm as a Language Parser (M363) by Jacob Katzenelson and Elie Milgrom. Realizability of Communication Nets: An Application of the Zadeh Criterion (M362) by Chuan Chen.
chunks.question: What topics are covered in reports M367 to M362?
chunks.answer: Amplifiers, transfer functions, martingales, SLIC, Markov algorithms, communication nets.

chunks.text: Functionals and Martingales of Wiener Process with a Two-dimensional Parameter (M361) by E. Wong and M. Zakai. The Hackenbush Number System For Compression of Numerical Data (M360) by E.R. Berlekamp. On Optimally Sparse Cycle and Coboundary Basis for a Linear Graph (M359) by L.O. Chua and L.K. Chen. Modeling the Step Response of Nonlinear Quasi-Static Systems (M358) by L.O. Chua and R.J. Schilling. A File Structure for Large Data Bases (M357) by Tung C. Chiang. A Historical Survey of Computational Methods (M356) by E. Polak.
chunks.question: What are the subjects of reports M361 to M356?
chunks.answer: Wiener processes, data compression, graph bases, nonlinear systems, databases, computational history.

chunks.text: A Property of the P-Medians of a Graph (M355) by Chuan Chen and C.K. Butt. The Primitive Recursive-Permutations Generate the Group of Recursive-Permutations (M354) by Ion S. Filotti. Reduction of the Grid Effects in Simulation Plasmas (M353) by Liu Chen, A. Bruce Langdon and C.K. Birdsall. Heating of Magnetized Plasmas by a Large-Amplitude Low-Frequency Electric Field (M352) by Liu Chen and C.K. Birdsall. On Minimal Triangulation of a Graph (M351) by T. Ohtsuki, L.K. Cheung and T. Fijisawa. Synthesis of Nonlinear Systems Having Prescribed Periodic Solutions (M350) by L.O. Chua and D.N. Green.
chunks.question: What do reports M355 to M350 focus on?
chunks.answer: Graph properties, permutations, plasma simulation, plasma heating, graph triangulation, nonlinear systems.

chunks.text: Short-term General Equilibrium in Space (M349) by Mario Ripper and Pravin Varaiya. Fasbol II: A Snobol4 Compiler for the PDP-10 (M348) by Paul Joseph Santos Jr. State Vectors: A Preliminary Survey (M347) by W.D. Maurer. Cancellations in Multivariable Continuous-Time and Discrete-Time Feedback Systems (M346) by C.A. Desoer and J.D. Schulman. A Martingale Approach to Point-Processes (M345) by Pierre M. Bremaud. A Theory of Algebraic N-Ports (M344) by L.O. Chua and Y-F Lam.
chunks.question: What are the key areas in reports M349 to M344?
chunks.answer: Spatial equilibrium, compilers, state vectors, feedback systems, point-processes, algebraic n-ports.

chunks.text: Optimal Cycles in Graphs and the Minimal Cost-to-time Ratio Problem (M343) by Eugene L. Lawler. Outline of a New Approach to the Analysis of Complex Systems and Decision Processes (M342) by L.A. Zadeh. Trends in the Theory of Decision-Making in Large Systems (M341) by Pravin Varaiya. Cutsets and Partitions of Hypergraphs (M340) by Eugene L. Lawler. On the Input-Output Properties of Convolution Feedback Systems (M338) by Frank M. Callier. Excitation Control of Synchronous Generations (M337) by Dale E. Mesple, Thomas K.K. Tam and Otto J. M. Smith.
chunks.question: What topics are addressed in reports M343 to M337?
chunks.answer: Graph cycles, complex systems analysis, decision-making, hypergraphs, feedback systems, excitation control.

chunks.text: A Fuzzy-Set-Theoretic Interpretation of Linguistic Hedges (M335) by L.A. Zadeh. Polynomial Bounded and (Apparently) Non Polynomial Bounded Matroid Computations (M332) by E.L. Lawler. Runoff: A Program for the Preparation of Documents (M331) by Richard Herzog and Nagib Badre. Effects of Congestion on the Shape of a City (M330) by L. Legey, Mario Ripper and Pravin Varaiya. Optimum City Size: A Minimum Congestion Cost Approach (M329) by D.A. Livesey. On Fuzzy Algorithms (M325) by L.A. Zadeh.
chunks.question: What are the main themes in reports M335 to M325?
chunks.answer: Fuzzy sets, matroid computations, document preparation, urban congestion, city size, fuzzy algorithms.

chunks.text: Trajectories of Nonlinear RLC Networks: A Geometric Approach (M324) by Charles A. Desoer and Felix F. Wu. Analysis of Computational Techniques for Circuit Theory (M323) by Hiromasa Haneda. A System-Theoretic View of Behavior Modification (M320) by L.A. Zadeh. Examples of Computer Aids to Algorithm Verification (M319) by W.D. Maurer. Theory and Practice of Algorithm Verification (M315) by W.D. Maurer.
chunks.question: What do the final reports M324 to M315 cover?
chunks.answer: RLC networks, circuit techniques, behavior modification, algorithm verification examples, verification theory.
